questions.question: In groups.tsx, are the Invite, Invite-link, and Manage-links controls still passed isDisabled={!isAdmin} (using the existing isAdmin boolean, now sourced from adminPubkeys.includes(ownPubkeyHex))? And is the pending-join-requests section still gated on isAdmin? Run grep -n 'isDisabled\|PendingRequestsSection\|isAdmin' app/pages/groups.tsx and confirm these gates are present and unchanged in structure (only the source of isAdmin has changed, not the gating logic). Show the relevant diff lines.

Is the accept/deny join-request action path unavailable to non-admins — not merely hidden behind a disabled button? Confirm that in groups.tsx, the handler for accept/deny is only wired up (or passed down) when isAdmin=true, so a non-admin page render produces no callable handler for these actions.

Is the cancel-pending-invitation action unavailable to non-admins in the wired-up groups.tsx? Confirm that the cancel handler is only passed/wired when isAdmin=true. Show the prop or callback that enables cancel and confirm it is conditional on isAdmin.

Does getPendingRemovals(groupId) get called on each groupDataVersion tick in groups.tsx, and is its result passed to MemberList as pendingRemovalPubkeys? Show the implementation: (a) the useEffect or useMemo dependency array includes groupDataVersion; (b) getPendingRemovals is called inside that effect/memo; (c) the result is stored in state or derived and passed to MemberList. On page reload and historical-sync replay, does re-observing kind-13 events rebuild pendingRemovalsRef so that getPendingRemovals returns the correct set and the pending badge re-renders?

Is adminPubkeys derived from mlsGroup?.groupData?.adminPubkeys ?? [] (never from a stale local variable) and is isCurrentUserAdmin derived from adminPubkeys.includes(ownPubkeyHex) (or equivalent hex-safe comparison)? Show the derivation lines in groups.tsx. Confirm these values update whenever mlsGroup updates (i.e., they are computed from the current mlsGroup in scope, not captured in a ref that is never updated).

Does the onMakeAdmin handler in groups.tsx show toast feedback for both success and failure? Show the handler implementation: (a) calls grantAdmin(groupId, pubkey); (b) on { ok: true }, shows a success toast; (c) on { ok: false, error }, shows an error toast with the error message. Confirm the handler is only passed to MemberList when isCurrentUserAdmin=true (not passed when the current user is not an admin).

Does groups.tsx remain a coordinator only — deriving state and wiring props — with no MLS commit logic or adminPubkeys mutation logic of its own? Confirm: (a) all MLS operations go through grantAdmin from MarmotContextValue; (b) adminPubkeys is read from mlsGroup.groupData, never written; (c) getPendingRemovals is a read-only call. Could groups.tsx be deleted and rewritten without modifying MemberList.tsx, LeaveGroupButton.tsx, or MarmotContext.tsx? If not, name the dependency.

Are the seam contracts from S3 (grantAdmin, getPendingRemovals), S4 (MemberList new props), and S5 (LeaveGroupButton new props) all satisfied at the call sites in groups.tsx? Verify: (a) MemberList receives adminPubkeys (string[]), isCurrentUserAdmin (boolean), onMakeAdmin (fn | undefined, conditional on isCurrentUserAdmin), and pendingRemovalPubkeys (string[]); (b) LeaveGroupButton receives adminPubkeys (string[]) and ownPubkeyHex (string | null). Show the JSX props for both component call sites.

Does groups.tsx compile with no TypeScript errors after adding the new props and handlers? Specifically: (a) does MemberList's updated prop type accept the new props without error; (b) does LeaveGroupButton's updated prop type accept the new props without error; (c) do grantAdmin and getPendingRemovals have the correct types as declared in MarmotContextValue? Run npx tsc --noEmit from app/ and show zero errors.

Is adminPubkeys derived inline in the render body (after the `if (!group) return null` guard), so it is always fresh from the current mlsGroup state? Confirm it is NOT captured in a useRef or stale closure. Check that `const adminPubkeys = mlsGroup?.groupData?.adminPubkeys ?? []` appears after the early-return guards and before the JSX return.

Does `getPendingRemovals(group.id)` in the render body re-evaluate on every render triggered by groupDataVersion changes? The MarmotContext effect increments groupDataVersion on every MLS tick, which causes GroupDetailView to re-render. Confirm that the synchronous `getPendingRemovals` call inside the render body gets fresh data on each such re-render without any additional memo or effect needed.

Is the removalPendingBadge copy key left defined (not deleted) in i18n.ts for both English and German, and does it carry a code comment explaining it is reserved for a future cause-distinction increment? Confirm the comment appears adjacent to the key in both language objects.
